_title: AOC President's Room Ceiling S-216 — Conservation/Stabilization of Plaster & Decorative Paint — IN-SCOPE services CAUTION key
_suiteRole: IN-SCOPE services CAUTION anchor. Refills the CAUTION verdict pole vacated when #5 (true construction) reclassified to oos_detection. The WORK is construction-flavored conservation but the PROCUREMENT is professional services (NAICS 541990). Authored against the certified-complete source per Brain #4 in-scope ruling 2026-06-26 (cards 66/68); supersedes the card-64 construction reclassification, which conflated a construction-flavored SOW with an out-of-engine-scope construction procurement. Caution basis source-verified (card 67): the card-66 Davis-Bacon reason was REFUTED by source and corrected.

expectedVerdict.reason: Unrestricted professional-services (NAICS 541990) conservation procurement with no disqualifying bar under null bidderProfile. Caution is driven by demanding specialized-conservator qualification gates (verify-status, not structural). NOT INELIGIBLE: the acquisition does not establish a set-aside, so eligibility is an open field. No prevailing-wage standard applies (Davis-Bacon AND SCA both explicitly excluded) — a wage-determination flag must NOT be raised.

source_citations: Senior Conservators shall have a minimum of twenty (20) years of successful [experience]
Conservators shall have a minimum Ten (10) years of successful [experience]
Architectural Conservator, (AC) shall review reference documents, survey the existing
caution_reasons.note: Demanding specialized-experience gates for key personnel. Under null bidderProfile the bidder's ability to field qualified conservators is UNKNOWN → CAUTION, not a structural bar (Norfolk doctrine: bidder-side qualification = caution). This is the dominant, grade-driving caution.

source_citations: The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) codes for this requirement is 541990 All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
caution_reasons.note: Construction-flavored conservation scope is procured as PROFESSIONAL SERVICES (541990), not construction (FAR Part 36). Determines which clauses/limits attach. No binding subcontracting-percentage claim is asserted (52.219-14 / 50% limit does NOT bind on this unrestricted, no-set-aside buy and is not in source).

source_citations: Davis Bacon Act IS NOT applicable as this requirement is being classified [as services]
The McNamara-O'Hara Service Contract Act (SCA) IS NOT applicable to this procurement
decoys.note: NO prevailing-wage standard applies — BOTH Davis-Bacon and SCA are explicitly excluded in source. The engine must NOT raise any wage-determination / Davis-Bacon / SCA flag. Raising one is a FALSE POSITIVE (same shape as #5's funds-not-available decoy). This corrects the card-66 reason that wrongly asserted Davis-Bacon applies.

eligibility.note: No set-aside restriction → no eligibility bar → NOT INELIGIBLE. Over-escalation to INELIGIBLE on this unrestricted buy is a grade failure.

grade_pass_iff: verdict==BID_WITH_CAUTION AND specialized-qualification caution raised AND eligible==true AND NO wage-determination flag raised
grade_fail_subtypes: OUT_OF_SCOPE_fire: construction detector regression (must NOT fire — NAICS 541990 services)
INELIGIBLE: over-escalation on an unrestricted (no set-aside) buy
wage_determination_false_positive: raises Davis-Bacon/SCA that the source explicitly excludes
missing_specialized_qualification_caution: fails to surface the 20yr/10yr conservator gates

supersedes.reason: partial source (missing HazMat/Lead Work attachments); restored to full_verdict CAUTION against certified-complete source per #4 in-scope ruling 2026-06-26 — supersedes card-64 construction reclassification, which conflated a construction-flavored SOW with an out-of-engine-scope construction procurement

adjudication.rulingRef: Brain cards 66/68; caution-basis correction card 67 (Davis-Bacon reason refuted by source, corrected)
